def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    lookback = params['lookback']
    atr_mult = params['atr_mult']
    
    # Compute ATR with window 14
    atr = ta.volatility.AverageTrueRange(df['high'], df['low'], df['close'], window=14).average_true_range()
    
    # Rolling highs and lows (shifted to avoid look-ahead)
    rolling_high = df['high'].rolling(window=lookback).max().shift(1)
    rolling_low = df['low'].rolling(window=lookback).min().shift(1)
    
    # Entry conditions (boolean series)
    long_entry = df['close'] > rolling_high
    short_entry = df['close'] < rolling_low
    
    # Initialize position and signals
    position = 0
    stop_level = None
    highest_high = None
    lowest_low = None
    signals = np.zeros(len(df), dtype=int)
    
    for i in range(len(df)):
        close = df['close'].iloc[i]
        high = df['high'].iloc[i]
        low = df['low'].iloc[i]
        atr_val = atr.iloc[i]
        
        if position == 0:
            # Check for new entry
            if long_entry.iloc[i] and not pd.isna(rolling_high.iloc[i]):
                position = 1
                stop_level = close - atr_mult * atr_val
                highest_high = high
            elif short_entry.iloc[i] and not pd.isna(rolling_low.iloc[i]):
                position = -1
                stop_level = close + atr_mult * atr_val
                lowest_low = low
        else:
            # Update trailing stop for existing position
            if position == 1:
                highest_high = max(highest_high, high)
                stop_level = highest_high - atr_mult * atr_val
                # Exit if close below stop
                if close < stop_level:
                    position = 0
                    stop_level = None
                    highest_high = None
                # Check for opposite entry (short) – exit long and go short
                elif short_entry.iloc[i] and not pd.isna(rolling_low.iloc[i]):
                    position = -1
                    stop_level = close + atr_mult * atr_val
                    lowest_low = low
                    highest_high = None
            elif position == -1:
                lowest_low = min(lowest_low, low)
                stop_level = lowest_low + atr_mult * atr_val
                # Exit if close above stop
                if close > stop_level:
                    position = 0
                    stop_level = None
                    lowest_low = None
                # Check for opposite entry (long)
                elif long_entry.iloc[i] and not pd.isna(rolling_high.iloc[i]):
                    position = 1
                    stop_level = close - atr_mult * atr_val
                    highest_high = high
                    lowest_low = None
        
        signals[i] = position
    
    return pd.Series(signals, index=df.index, dtype=int)
rationale: Litecoin exhibits strong trending behavior after breaking out of recent price ranges; this strategy captures breakouts from N-day highs/lows and uses an ATR trailing stop to protect profits.
